<!--
Auto-loaded by GitHub when opening a PR. Keep it short — the goal is to
prompt the author to think through what changed + how it was verified, not
to gatekeep with a wall of checkboxes.

Drop the sections that don't apply to your change.
-->

## What

<!-- 1-2 sentences. Focus on the user-visible or behavioral change, not the file list (the diff covers the file list). -->

## Why

<!-- The motivation. Link to an issue, a Lighthouse report, a Rich Results screenshot, or a Slack/email thread when relevant. -->

## How tested

<!--
- `npm run audit:all` green (the 25-gate verification chain)?
- Local dev server smoke for UI changes?
- Manual checks for content/CMS edits (CMS preview, Rich Results, LinkedIn Post Inspector, Lighthouse)?
-->

## Type

<!-- pick one -->

- [ ] `feat` — user-visible new functionality
- [ ] `fix` — bug fix
- [ ] `refactor` — internal change, behavior unchanged
- [ ] `perf` — performance improvement
- [ ] `docs` — documentation only
- [ ] `chore` — build / CI / tooling / config
- [ ] `test` — tests only
- [ ] `style` — formatting only (no semantic change)

## Audit checklist coverage

<!--
Optional. If this PR touches an area tracked in AUDIT_CHECKLIST.md, note
which rows it advances. Example: "Closes 13.17 (post-deploy smoke)" or
"Updates 18.11 (navbar brand) — adds dark logo variant".
-->
